identity.name: Nikolaos Nikolaou
identity.tagline: Senior XR Developer · Game Master · Maker
identity.classChain: Senior XR Developer 6 / Game Master 7 / Maker 5 / Painter 3
identity.bio: Senior Unity gameplay programmer with 6+ years of high-performance C# architecture and polished player interactions. Master of strict VR/XR technical requirements — spatial UI, 3D math, optimization for mobile. I build modular, decoupled systems that support rapid iteration and large-scale content delivery. I turn complex requirements into intuitive, responsive gameplay. Self-driven collaborator committed to technical excellence and meticulous refinement of the player experience.
identity.adventurerIntro: Builds gameplay systems for a living, runs tabletop campaigns for the love of it, and finishes Grey Knights one model at a time. He'll out-argue you about three things at once and somehow still ship four.
identity.backstory: Greek by birth, Copenhagen by choice. Currently building a TTRPG system on the side. Probably has more hobbies than fingers.
identity.sheetInvite: This is an interactive site — poke around, press things, see what happens. Probably your only chance to get a look behind the GM's screen, or be encouraged to break a stranger's website. Take it.

traits.name: Hyperfocus
traits.desc: Locks onto a problem until it's solved. Forgets meals. Worth it.

traits.name: Break-and-Repurpose
traits.desc: Uses tools against their design. Often it works. Sometimes it works better.

traits.name: Think Fast
traits.desc: Reasons through what'll work before building. Rejects 'fail fast' as wasteful.

traits.name: Anticipate Failure
traits.desc: Names the failure modes before they fail. The future thanks him.

traits.name: Adversarial Communicator
traits.desc: Defends the non-obvious. Challenges the consensus. Makes features fight for their right to be built.

traits.name: ADHD (Probably)
traits.desc: Multiple hobbies, multiple obsessions, one extremely tidy git history.

bullets: Designed custom Unity tools that accelerated prototyping and shortened design-feedback loops.
Led high-fidelity training simulations with strict modular architecture and performance budgets.
Owned performance profiling and debugging for heavy scenes — kept everything smooth.
Mentored junior developers and reviewed code to raise team production standards.
Spearheaded engineering on an SXSW-award-winning project — translated abstract design into C# mechanics.
Built development templates and standardized assets that streamlined studio workflow.
Worked tight loops with artists and designers — bridged visual fidelity and technical constraints.
Developed on-location AR with high-precision tracking and custom interaction logic.
Built interactive training simulations for healthcare education in Denmark and East Africa.
Sole programmer at the start of 'Foxtale', an Unreal narrative game — owned gameplay systems from concept to a DFI-funded vertical slice.
Built the vertical slice that secured the studio's Danish Film Institute (DFI) grant.
Implemented character controllers, interaction systems, game states — responsive, bug-free demo.
Foxtale was inspired by 'Hyelight', his own Unity diploma project where he was sole coder and developer.
Left in good standing in 2021, once the grant was secured — no involvement since. Foxtale later released on Steam as 'Yokai Tales: Fox'.

showcases.title: Grit & Glory
showcases.tagline: A roleplaying game of survival, steel, and scars.

coverHero.tagline: A Roleplaying Game of Survival, Steel, and Scars
teaser: After the gods withdrew and the Shatter cracked the sky, the world kept turning — colder, hungrier, and still worth fighting for.

Grit & Glory is a tabletop RPG of earned competence and real consequence. Build a dice pool from what you've trained and watch yourself go thin under pressure. Watch your gear wear down, blow by blow, one die-step at a time.
coverHero.byline: Designed and Written by Nikos Nikolaou · 2026

story: Grit & Glory grew out of a six-year Faerûn campaign that kept outgrowing the rules holding it together. D&D's HP bloat didn't match the wounds the table was telling stories about; levels arrived faster than the world's economy could absorb; the players kept inventing — from whole cloth — the mechanics they wanted to see. Eventually I stopped patching and started writing my own system.

The system is built on three locked feelings. The Grind: every action carries weight; nothing is free. The Welcome Bruise: the only band of outcome that delivers per-roll advancement is the one where you almost made it; growth is paid for in scars. Lawful Brutality: no hidden target numbers; the world is hostile, but it tells you exactly how hard it's pushing back, in the currency of dice.

Sessions feel like pressure — every roll spends something: focus, gear, daylight, blood. Campaigns feel like becoming. Who your character is at session ten isn't who they were at session one, because something happened on the road, and something stayed.
Heroism isn't a birthright here. Hope is earned. So is glory.

Currently in writing. First-edition draft sits at 387 pages, the bestiary grows teeth every time I declare it finished, and playtest reports live in their own folder so design decisions stay auditable years from now.

showcases.title: 3D Print & Paint
showcases.story: Started with one printer. Ended up with a small company for a while. Resin for fine detail, FDM for terrain and brackets. I paint the minis I print — Grey Knights for 40k, Rohan for Middle-earth Strategy Battle Game, and one-off characters for whoever's at the table.

showcases.title: Homebrew Faerûn
showcases.tagline: Six years of GM notes — the players' fault, mostly
story: Six-plus years running Faerûn — a mix of homebrew arcs and prebuilt campaigns modified past recognition, because the table never wants to do the thing the book expects. Four campaigns have actually reached an ending, which, if you've run D&D, you know is the real boss fight: 'A Series of Unfortunate Rolls', 'And It All Went to Hell', 'The Blue Plague', and 'Until Death Do Us Apart'.

They tell you what kind of GM he is. 'And It All Went to Hell' — Elturel and its second sun torn screaming into Avernus, the party racing a collapsing skyline to a crater where a city used to be. 'Until Death Do Us Apart' — a warlord who takes Shar's curse of vampirism not for power but to outlive the universe and find the one love fate keeps killing, lifetime after lifetime. Read-aloud handouts, Alaundo's prophecies, a whole homebrew mythology where Loki, the Raven Queen, and the Lord of Light seal a death-goddess in iron.

Underneath the stories is a rules problem he kept solving. He keeps a homebrewery — a curated stack of other people's house rules he likes, plus his own: the Blood Sorcerer and Red Wizard subclasses are his. The whole house ruleset even has a name; it started as 'The Two Wolves' before it became 'Until Death Do Us Apart', and it's what he runs most games on.

That ruleset is the tell. Patch enough of a system and one day you notice you're not patching anymore — you're writing a new one. That's the exact moment Grit & Glory started. Canon doesn't survive contact with the party; turns out, neither do the rules.

showcases.title: Khora — XR Work
story: Lead XR developer at Khora — Copenhagen's XR studio — from 2021 to 2025. Khora builds VR and AR for whoever walks in: hospitals, museums, trade unions, the National Museum, a parachute association. The brief changed every few weeks and the device budget never got more generous. I shipped across all of it.

The healthcare and training cluster is where the stakes lived. A neonatal-resuscitation VR simulation — quiz, sandbox, and timed skill drills — that piloted with 45 health workers across three hospitals in Pemba, Tanzania, under EU Horizon's XR2LEARN. Conflict-handling VR for Ambulance Syd paramedics, branching films where the wrong de-escalation ends the scenario. Robot-handling training for Industriens Uddannelser. A mobility game for people with cerebral palsy that re-tuned itself to each player's reach.

The heritage and empathy cluster is where the craft lived. On-site AR that rebuilt Esrum Monastery on a tablet and let visitors watch it fall. A VR climb up Nikolaj Kunsthal's tower while it was closed for renovation. VR Qayak for the Nuuk museum — kayaking, drum dance, Greenlandic history. A 360 time-travel through Copenhagen's Rådhuspladsen for Nationalmuseet. And quieter, heavier pieces — 'With These Hands', survivor testimony you hold in your own hands — where a dropped frame breaks the spell entirely.

Underneath the variety, the same engineering discipline every time: strict budgets on Quest 2, Pico, and Oculus Go; high-precision tracking for on-location AR; studio-wide templates and asset standards so the next project didn't start from zero. Clients changed their minds, scopes drifted, hardware shipped late — and the thing went out the door anyway. One of them came back with an SXSW nod.
milestones: Lead XR developer at Khora, 2021–2025

Studio-wide dev templates and asset standards

showcases.title: Novo — XR for Healthcare
showcases.story: Senior XR developer on high-fidelity VR training simulations for healthcare. Most of the work lives under NDA, so specifics stay quiet — but the shape of it: custom Unity tooling that shortened design-feedback loops, performance profiling and debugging passes that kept heavy scenes smooth, and modular architecture under strict performance budgets. Also reviewed code and mentored junior developers so the team's output kept rising.

showcases.title: Silkroad — Foxtale (now Yokai Tales: Fox)
showcases.tagline: Sole Programmer / CTO · Unreal · DFI-funded · 2019–2021 · departed 2021
story: Silkroad Studios, 2019 to 2021: sole programmer and CTO, in at the start of 'Foxtale' — an Unreal narrative game. He owned the gameplay engineering and took it from concept to a DFI-funded vertical slice. Two years holding up the engineering of a small studio while wearing a title he still won't say out loud unless you ask twice.

Foxtale didn't come from nowhere. It was inspired by 'Hyelight', his own university diploma project — a Unity game he built solo as the only coder and developer. The images on this page are Hyelight; the Behance link below is the full project. That's the work that set everything here in motion.

He built the vertical slice that secured the studio's Danish Film Institute grant — and then he left in 2021. To be clear: he's on good terms with Silkroad, but he's had no involvement in the project since. The engineering groundwork and the grant were his; everything Foxtale became after 2021 belongs to the team that stayed.
Curious what it became? Foxtale was renamed and released on Steam as 'Yokai Tales: Fox' — a hand-painted narrative adventure, years of other people's work after his time. Wishlist link below.
milestones: Sole programmer + CTO at Silkroad Studios, 2019–2021
In at the start of 'Foxtale', an Unreal narrative game
Took it from concept to a DFI-funded vertical slice
Built the slice that secured the DFI (Danish Film Institute) grant
Inspired by 'Hyelight', his solo Unity diploma project (the images here)
Left in good standing in 2021 — no involvement since
Foxtale released on Steam as 'Yokai Tales: Fox' (post-departure work)

showcases.title: Medialogy — Comfort XR
story: Master's thesis at Aalborg University, Copenhagen: pain distraction in VR for needle procedures — specifically K-wire insertions and epidural needle work. The hard cases. Patient awake, fully conscious, and the worst of it is anticipation: watching the needle, bracing for it. The question was whether presence could pull attention off the needle entirely.

Cartoons are passive; you can look away. VR can demand you look, reach, and act somewhere else while the procedure happens. The build became the seed of Comfort XR — an immersive distraction layer that drops you somewhere more interesting than the table and keeps you there through the moment that usually triggers the panic.

It's the foundational piece — the proof that the method worked on real procedural pain, not just nerves. The same instinct scaled up into the XR-for-healthcare work that followed. Comfort XR later extended to a softer audience entirely — kids at the dentist — reaching real clinics with Rigshospitalet and Ringkøbing-Skjern Kommune.
milestones: MSc Medialogy — Aalborg University, Copenhagen
Thesis: VR pain distraction for K-wire and epidural needle procedures
Active immersion vs. needle anticipation in conscious patients
Seeded Comfort XR — the XR-for-healthcare path started here
Comfort XR later extended to pediatric dentistry (Rigshospitalet & Ringkøbing-Skjern)

showcases.title: Aegean — Big Chimera
showcases.tagline: MSc Products and Systems Design Engineering · University of the Aegean, Syros
story: Master's thesis at the University of the Aegean, on Syros, February 2019: a location-aware mobile AR serious game for protecting the architectural and artistic heritage of Ermoupolis — the island's neoclassical capital. Built in Unity on Mapbox: real GPS and the actual streets of Ermoupolis, so the game knew where you physically stood and walked you between real heritage buildings.

The hook — and the name — comes from a book. Big Chimera ties M. Karagatsis's novel 'Η Μεγάλη Χίμαιρα' (The Great Chimera) to the real place it's set in. As you walk Ermoupolis, the app overlays the novel's scenes onto the exact buildings where they happen: the couple's arrival at the harbor aboard the ship Chimera, the wedding at Agios Nikolaos, the receptions in the shipowners' mansions. Story and city become the same layer.

It was built as a serious game, not a guided tour — players don't just see the heritage, they learn why it matters and why it needs protecting. The whole thing was shaped by rapid prototyping and round after round of user feedback: build, test on real people walking the real town, redesign, repeat, then evaluate the final app empirically.

Before the thesis came the internship, filed under AReal — an alternate-reality game: transmedia, real-world narrative, puzzles a community solves in real time. It surfaced as 'Inside Out' with Cat White Studios at the Eve's Walk Festival on Syros. Location-based, serious games, AR, GPS, Unity — every thread that became the XR career started here, on a Greek island, in a language he defended a thesis in.
milestones: MSc Products & Systems Design Engineering — University of the Aegean, Syros (Feb 2019)
Thesis: a location-aware mobile AR serious game for the heritage of Ermoupolis, defended in Greek
Ties M. Karagatsis's novel 'The Great Chimera' to the real streets where it's set
Built in Unity + Mapbox — real GPS, routing, and Ermoupolis geography in 3D
Designed and evaluated through rapid prototyping + continuous user feedback
Internship: AReal / 'Inside Out' ARG — Cat White Studios, Eve's Walk Festival (Syros)
